revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Akeai Kofun]][[ja:明合古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Akeae Kofun | native_name =明合古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Akeai Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akeae Kofun | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Akeae Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.767431|N|136.439606|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsu, Mie|en|Tsu, Mie|qid=Q203027|lt=Tsu, Mie|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Akeae Kofun '''|明合古墳||qq=}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ano neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tsu, Mie |en|Tsu, Mie |qid=Q203027|lt=Tsu|1=Tsu, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1952.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1522 |title=明合古墳 |trans-title=Akeae Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Akeae Kofun is located at the eastern end of a low hill with an elevation of 40 meters extending from the eastern foot of Kyogamine in the middleof the Ano River in central Mie Prefecture. It is an extremely unusual {{nihongo|''Sōhōchū-hofun''|双方中方墳||qq=}}-type tumulus, which is shaped like an hourglass when viewed from above, consisting of trapezoidal mounds that extend from either side of a large square central mound. Cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', shield-shaped ''haniwa'', house-shaped ''haniwa'', and other figurative ''haniwa'' were excavated from the top and upper tier of the main Kofun, which date its construction to the first half of the middleKofun period (first half of the 5th century AD). <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref>''[[Fukiishi]]'' have also been found on the surface of the mound, and fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have also been discovered. The tumulus was also once surrounded by a moat. However, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are not known, as the interior of tumulus has never been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]]. The ''kofun'' was discovered in 1945. It is one of a cluster of eight tumuli that were once in this area, of which this tumulus and five smaller ones survive. The area is now open to the public as the {{nihongo|Akeae Kofun Historic Park|明合古墳歴史公園|Akeae Kofun Rekishi Kōen|qq=}} ;Total length: 81.1 meters: ;Central portion: 60.0 x 48.3 meters x 1.7 to 2.5 meters (lower tier) / 42 x 15 meters x 6.0 to 7.0 meters (upper tier) ;North extension: 10.0 meters long x 17.4 meters wide x 1.8 meters high ;South extension: 13.0 meters ling x 21 meters wide x 1.7 to 20 meters high ==Gallery== <gallery> File:Akeai Kofun, kita-tsukuridashi.jpg|North extension File:Akeai Kofun, minami-tsukuridashi.jpg|south extension File:Akeai Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|central portion file:明合古墳出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|fragments of ''haniwa'' </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|qid=Q20983066|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== [[Category:broken commons categories]] *[https://www.tsukanko.jp/spot/101/ Tsu city tourist information site] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.bunka.pref.mie.lg.jp/bunkazai/da/daItemDetail?mngnum=730373&pageCur=5&pub_id=1 Cultural properties of Mie Prefecture] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} Akeai Kofun
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
